commands.repair_reason: - Maintainers should decide whether the deterministic helper proof is sufficient for this session-state recovery change and then proceed through normal mergeability/check handling.; Cleared: The diff only changes TypeScript agent helper/test code and does not add dependency, workflow, credential, install, or supply-chain surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR trims trailing assistant messages from compaction-timeout recovery snapshots on both pre-compaction and current fallback paths and adds focused helper regressions.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +30. Total +38 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source/helper boundary: current main can select a timed-out snapshot ending in `assistant`, and `agentLoopContinue` rejects assistant-tail context. I did not run a live long-session provider/channel timeout.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainers should decide whether the deterministic helper proof is sufficient for this session-state recovery change and then proceed through normal mergeability/check handling.

target.title: fix(agents): trim assistant-tail compaction snapshots
target.body:
Fixes #87472.

## Summary
- trim trailing assistant turns from compaction-timeout recovery snapshots before they are handed downstream for retry/replay decisions
- preserve the underlying persisted transcript; only the selected recovery snapshot is normalized
- add regressions for both pre-compaction snapshot selection and current-snapshot fallback

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Compaction-timeout recovery must not hand downstream retry/replay code a transcript whose last message has `role: assistant`; that shape causes `Cannot continue from message role: assistant` on provider fallback/continuation.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-87472`, branch `fix/compaction-timeout-assistant-tail-87472`, Node via repo scripts, dependencies installed with `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm exec tsx -e ...` against the real `selectCompactionTimeoutSnapshot` helper with a pre-compaction snapshot ending in an assistant turn, then ran the requested vitest suites.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "source": "pre-compaction",
  "sessionIdUsed": "session-pre",
  "messageCount": 1,
  "tailRole": "user",
  "assistantTailPresent": false
}
```

Additional command output:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/compaction-timeout.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.timeout-triggered-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts` -> 5 test files passed, 258 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` -> 3 test files passed, 244 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` -> 2 test files passed, 64 tests passed.

Observed result after fix: The selected compaction-timeout recovery snapshot keeps the pre-compaction session but trims the already-delivered assistant tail, so the recovered snapshot ends on the user turn instead of an assistant turn.

What was not tested: I did not run a live Anthropic/Discord long-session compaction timeout because the bug path is deterministic at the snapshot-selection boundary and the provider failure requires a long timed-out compaction scenario.

## Author attribution
If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution for `Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>` or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.author: Alix-007

target.title: test(imessage): cover remote media staging order
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds regression coverage for path-only remote iMessage media.
- Verifies remote media is staged before media understanding sees the attachment path.
- Verifies the late staging path is not invoked a second time after the pre-understanding stage succeeds.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Current `main` stages remote iMessage media before media understanding; this PR adds regression coverage for the path-only remote-media case that used to be easy to miss.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux at PR head `09ba0d041b23ccb7fc5614c82b204a45683bcc8d`, with workspace dependencies installed.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts -t "stages remote inbound media before media understanding" --reporter=verbose`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/openclaw-issue-87089

✓ |auto-reply-reply| src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig message hooks > stages remote inbound media before media understanding 14655ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 9 skipped (10)
Duration  19.57s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The added path-only remote iMessage regression test executed at the PR head and passed; the test asserts staging happens before media understanding and that the late staging path is not called a second time.

**What was not tested**: A live iMessage gateway transfer on macOS; this PR is scoped to regression coverage for the current OpenClaw reply pipeline behavior.

## Supplemental checks
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.message-hooks.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
